title: Multi-session longitudinal motor imagery EEG dataset (Kumar et al. 2024)
description: A multi-session longitudinal motor imagery EEG dataset from 18 BCI-naive subjects performing left and right hand motor imagery tasks across 6 sessions. The dataset demonstrates that decoders trained on expert data are readily transferable to inexperienced users through domain adaptation techniques, enabling reduced-calibration BCI training via transfer learning. Two real-time frameworks—Generic Recentering (unsupervised) and Personally Assisted Recentering (supervised)—were evaluated for promoting individual BCI skill acquisition.
methods_description: EEG data were recorded from 18 healthy subjects at 512 Hz using 22 EEG channels and 3 EOG channels with an ANT Neuro eego mylab system. Subjects performed cue-based left/right hand motor imagery tasks across 6 sessions (1 offline + 5 online) with 20 trials per session (10 per class). Features were extracted as covariance matrices in the 8-30 Hz frequency band and classified using Riemannian Minimum Distance to Mean (MDM) decoder. Subjects were divided into two groups: Generic Recentering (N=9, unsupervised domain adaptation) and Personally Assisted Recentering (N=9, supervised recalibration).
